recommendation: Add a new answer-format prompt candidate through the existing prompt_variant hook, but classify it as proposal-only until a later scored run. The current path is already suitable: scripts/run_experiment_worker.py prompt_sweep entries pass prompt_variant params into RAGPipeline, src/rag.py forwards prompt_variant to generate_answer, and src/generator.py resolves variants through PROMPT_VARIANTS/get_system_prompt. The lowest-risk experiment hook is therefore a new generator prompt constant plus a PROMPT_VARIANTS key, and a new prompt_sweep suite row such as prompt_answer_format_safety. Do not edit defaults or claim score improvement from no-api validation.

suggested_suite_entries.purpose: Compare an answer-format safety prompt against default, strict_evidence, and concise_verified under the same control retrieval settings.
Keep the existing baseline unchanged so a later scored run can separate prompt-only effects from retrieval changes.

no_api_validation_checks: Static import/compile check for src/generator.py and src/rag.py after adding the prompt constant and PROMPT_VARIANTS key.
No-API unit-style check that get_system_prompt('answer_format_safety') returns the new prompt and get_system_prompt('unknown') still falls back to SYSTEM_PROMPT.

No-API monkeypatch check of src.generator._client.chat.completions.create to assert generate_answer(..., prompt_variant='answer_format_safety') places the new system prompt in messages[0] without making a network call.

No-API monkeypatch check of src.rag.generate_answer/retrieve/rewrite_query to assert RAGPipeline(params={'prompt_variant':'answer_format_safety'}).ask(...) forwards prompt_variant exactly once and preserves the existing result shape.

Dry-run run_experiment_worker invocation for prompt_sweep with --dry-run --max-experiments limited after the suite row is added, verifying results.csv/details.json/worker_output.json serialize the new experiment row without touching final logs.
Confirm no default-control mutation: DEFAULT_PARAMS prompt_variant remains default, CONTROL values remain unchanged, and baseline_default emits no prompt_variant override.
future_scored_eval_plan: Run the updated prompt_sweep on the current tune question set with judge enabled and the same retrieval controls as the existing prompt candidates.
Treat the new prompt candidate as adopted only if EDD improves or is statistically tied while answer quality review shows better directness, citation clarity, source-scope discipline, or concision.
Run the candidate on holdout/validation separately from tune; do not reuse targeted retry scores as final generalization evidence.

Review case-level failures before promotion, especially source-scope bleed, false abstention, missed abstention, overlong answers, unsupported details, and answers that hide missing fields behind vague phrasing.
If latency changes, report it as observed secondary behavior only; the prompt hook should not be bundled with adaptive_top_k or retrieval parameter changes in the same scored claim.
Record first_validation_score separately from measurement corrections or targeted reruns, following the loop_points.csv distinction between first evidence and recomputation/diagnostic points.
risks: A format-focused prompt can improve apparent readability while lowering evidence fit if it encourages summarization beyond retrieved chunks.
A stricter missing-field instruction can increase false abstention on answerable cases unless evaluated case by case.
Adding the suite row without a no-api dry run could break worker CSV/JSON output shape even before any model call.
Changing SYSTEM_PROMPT or DEFAULT_PARAMS would confound prompt-candidate scoring with baseline behavior.
Claiming improvement from no-api checks, dry-run rows, or targeted examples would repeat the documented risk of mixing diagnostics with scored validation.

The later scored run may be affected by judge calibration issues already called out in next_improvement_tasks.md, so blind calibration or stricter unsupported-audit review should remain part of promotion criteria.
